rationales.hedonism: He centered the day on immediate enjoyment: sleeping in, making French press coffee, reading on the porch, and saying "the break is nice" and "that's enough."
He described a week at a cabin with "no cell service for five days" as the "best decision we've made in months," framing the trip as a deliberately pleasurable escape.
rationales.benevolence: He emphasized that this was a choice shared "with Jenna" and called it "the best decision we've made," showing investment in a close relationship.

rationales.self_direction: He chose his own boundary over institutional pressure, saying "I'm not doing it this year" and deciding to ignore the email until August.
rationales.hedonism: He defended "protect[ing] their own time" and refusing to spend July reworking curriculum, prioritizing leisure and relief over extra work.
rationales.conformity: He openly rejected the expected response to the supposedly "optional" sessions, refusing to go along with a norm that colleagues comply with.

rationales.stimulation: He chose a more involved, novel challenge by making paella "from scratch" for four hours because the demanding process itself was part of the appeal.
rationales.hedonism: He said "the four hours weren't a cost - they were the point" and described savoring the sensory ritual of cooking, showing clear pursuit of present enjoyment.

rationales.self_direction: He independently raised an issue in the staff meeting because "it felt dishonest not to say something," acting on his own judgment despite poor support.
rationales.conformity: He spoke up after others stayed silent and then felt like he had "spoken out of turn," showing willingness to violate the room's expected deference.
rationales.universalism: He pushed for curriculum that included "different cultures" because many students' families came from Syria, Turkey, or Eritrea, showing concern for inclusion beyond his immediate circle.
He was distressed by "microplastics found in baby formula" and by systems that "are supposed to protect people" but do not, showing strong concern for vulnerable people and public welfare.

rationales.benevolence: He spent time after school helping Amira with reading and celebrated her finishing a chapter book, finding meaning in helping one child directly.

rationales.conformity: He "just nodded and ate" instead of challenging his father, explicitly choosing restraint to avoid conflict in the relationship.
rationales.benevolence: He stayed quiet mainly to protect "the version of our relationship where we do not argue," showing care for a close family bond.
rationales.universalism: He focused on pollution in the Scheldt and said "the river matters and the people who fish in it matter and the kids who swim in it matter," expressing broad concern for environmental and public wellbeing.

He framed his constant concern as "paying attention" to issues like "ocean acidification" and going out of his way to shop organic, showing sustained commitment to environmental and wider social concerns.

rationales.benevolence: He spent the afternoon sorting donated winter coats and embraced Fatima's idea that "you help because the person in front of you is cold," emphasizing direct care for others nearby.
rationales.universalism: The volunteering was tied to helping resettled families and meeting basic needs for people beyond his immediate circle, showing concern for broader human welfare.

rationales.self_direction: He said "I stopped waiting for permission" and created the Friday question time on his own, acting autonomously inside the institution.
rationales.conformity: He deliberately acted with "no approval needed," stepping outside the usual expectation that he wait for institutional permission.
rationales.benevolence: He used class time to help children "make sense of things" and described that work with six-year-olds as the part that feels right, showing care for the kids directly in front of him.
rationales.universalism: The questions he opened space for, like why some people do not have houses and whether fish feel pain, centered wider social and ethical concern beyond personal interests.

rationales.self_direction: He said he would make the same point again because he does not want to "stay quiet when it matters," showing commitment to acting on his own convictions.
rationales.conformity: He challenged a colleague at lunch even though it made the conversation "awkward," showing willingness to disrupt harmony rather than go along.
rationales.universalism: He insisted climate content belongs in school because "the climate is not political, it is physical reality," reflecting concern for broader environmental truth and collective welfare.

rationales.self_direction: He deliberately used "the tracking sheets I designed back in 2019 instead of the new corporate software," relying on his own judgment over imposed procedure.
rationales.achievement: He stayed late to sign off on the shipment reports himself because he wanted "the numbers" to be right, showing strong focus on competence and performance.
rationales.power: He kept control by refusing to delegate to Arturo and insisting on checking the reports "the way I always check them."
rationales.conformity: He ignored the mandated tool and quietly kept doing things his own way, rather than following the expected corporate process.

rationales.self_direction: He described a consistent strategy of deciding for himself which parts of the initiative actually apply locally and keeping only what is useful.
rationales.security: His resolution was to "keep doing what works," and he justified selectively ignoring changes because "the numbers stay good," prioritizing stable, proven methods.
rationales.conformity: He openly described sitting through presentations and then "quietly go[ing] back to what actually functions," showing resistance to imposed norms.

rationales.self_direction: He rejected the "one-size-fits-all approach" and insisted each warehouse has its own exceptions, defending local judgment over centralized control.
rationales.achievement: He gathered data showing a "94% on-time delivery rate" and set a performance-based standard for whether the new software deserved adoption.
rationales.conformity: He coordinated with Sergio to document failures and justify "doing something different" instead of simply complying with the regional directive.

rationales.self_direction: He felt his independent judgment was being overridden, saying "My numbers did not matter" and that they wanted to replace what works with uniformity.
rationales.achievement: He and Sergio "stayed late preparing the presentation" and he emphasized the effort he put into building a strong case with numbers.
rationales.power: He described having built something effective over "Twenty-five years" only to have the decision imposed from above, highlighting loss of influence and control.

rationales.self_direction: He manually overrode the software because local realities required it and insisted that what matters is solutions grounded in how the facility actually works.
rationales.power: He was frustrated that "nobody asked us before deciding" and that decisions were simply "handed down," showing felt loss of voice and control.
rationales.conformity: He explicitly rejected a culture where they want "compliance, not solutions," resisting the expectation to simply follow the imposed system.
